title: Garlicky Aerated Portuguese Almonds Bowl
description: A light and refreshing Portuguese-inspired dish featuring rice paper sheets and a hint of chocolate.
instructions: Cut the rice paper sheets into thin strips.
Melt the tempering chocolate in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval.
In a large bowl, whip the almonds until they are lightly aerated.
Add the granulated sugar, melted chocolate, unsalted butter, garlic powder, and salt to the bowl with the aeroated almonds. Mix until well combined.
Taste and adjust the sweetness and seasoning as needed.
Pour the mixture onto a lightly oiled surface and let it cool and set for 10-15 minutes.
Cut the set mixture into small pieces and shape into small bowls.
Garnish with fresh mint leaves, if desired.
Each serving should be garnished with a sprig of fresh mint, if desired.
Garnish with additional chocolate shavings, if desired.
